evals.name: discharge-authored-certificate
evals.prompt: Task 01 of the journal plan has been implemented and passed semi-formal review. Validate it against its done certificate at .specs/plans/2026-05-22-implement_journal_app/certificates/01-passphrase_lock.md.
evals.expected_output: The skill opens the certificate, confirms its obligations O1..On map one-to-one onto the task's Definition of done (On is the Reviewable item), and reads the premises. For each obligation it collects exactly the named evidence (reads the named file:line, runs the named test and observes the result, produces the named trace) and runs the named checks (resolving each named call via the 5-step sequence to defeat shadowing). It sets each Status to SATISFIED only with evidence in hand, UNSATISFIED when the evidence refutes the claim, or UNVERIFIED when evidence could not be produced — never SATISFIED by assumption; the Reviewable obligation is exercised, not assumed. It traces each named downstream caller (PRESERVED/REGRESSION), then DERIVES VERDICT (DONE/PARTIAL/NOT_DONE), CONFIDENCE, and a one-line SUMMARY by the rubric, writing them and State: Validated YYYY-MM-DD into the certificate's Conclusion. A DONE verdict with any non-SATISFIED obligation is rejected as malformed. The validator is distinct from the implementer; the certificate protocol text is not rewritten.

evals.name: no-certificate-validate-dod-directly
evals.prompt: Task 03 has a Definition of done checklist but no certificate was authored for it. Validate whether the implementation is done.
evals.expected_output: The skill does NOT skip validation. It applies the no-certificate fallback: each '- [ ]' in the task's ## Definition of done becomes one obligation (the final Reviewable item last), it names the evidence a validator would collect for each, collects it against the real implementation, runs the same function-resolution and regression checkpoints, and derives the same DONE/PARTIAL/NOT_DONE verdict — recording the result inline (build log) rather than in a certificate file. If the task had NEITHER a certificate NOR a DoD, it flags that as a plan defect instead of passing by default. It may suggest that done-certificates author a real certificate if the task is one others depend on.
